payload.title: Joint Minutes of the Metropolitan Transportation Authority, the New York City Transit Authority, the Manhattan and Bronx Surface Transit Operating Authority, the Staten Island Rapid Transit Operating Authority, the Metropolitan Suburban Bus Authority, the Triborough Bridge and Tunnel Authority, the Long Island Rail Road Company, Metro-North Commuter Railroad Company, MTA Construction and Development Company, the MTA Bus Company, Grand Central Madison Operating Company, and the First Mutual Transportation Assurance Company

payload.claim_text: MTA is about to implement a 4% fare increase on January 4
payload.statement: MTA is about to implement a 4% fare increase on January 4
payload.subject: MTA fare increase

warnings: claim.statement is accepted as passthrough, but prefer claim_text. Claim statement.

payload.claim_text: MTA has a balanced budget for 2026
payload.statement: MTA has a balanced budget for 2026
payload.subject: MTA budget

warnings: claim.statement is accepted as passthrough, but prefer claim_text. Claim statement.

payload.context: Looking at a $2.5 billion annual deficit, representing about 14% of the budget
payload.raw_value_text: $2.5 billion annual deficit, representing about 14% of the budget

payload.description: $400 million package of efficiencies committed in two-year period
payload.raw_value_text: $400 million package of efficiencies in a two-year period

payload.description: MTA is hitting $500 million in savings in 2025

payload.claim_text: Phase 1 of the cost savings program achieved $500 million in savings through a range of improvements, for example, adjustments to the frequency of rail car maintenance based on data analysis. MTA is using technology to optimize railroad and crew scheduling. MTA increased energy efficiencies by installing LED lighting all over the system, and is evaluating usage patterns on computer systems to eliminate wasteful software licenses. Additionally, MTA renegotiated deals with cell phone carriers, and removed cell phones and other electronic devices not in use. Further, MTA is realizing savings from the transition to tap-and-ride
payload.statement: Phase 1 of the cost savings program achieved $500 million in savings
payload.subject: MTA cost savings Phase 1

warnings: claim.statement is accepted as passthrough, but prefer claim_text. Claim statement.

payload.description: Shooting for another $250 million in annual efficiency savings
payload.raw_value_text: another $250 million in annual efficiency savings
2027 deficit, which was $345 million back in July, to be $160 million
payload.description: November Financial Plan projects the 2027 deficit reduced from $345 million to $160 million

payload.raw_value_text: more than $2 billion in cost savings over the next four years
$300+ million a year on operating Grand Central Madison
additional $35 million invested in the Queens bus system as a result of the bus network redesign
payload.description: additional $35 million invested in the Queens bus system as a result of the bus network redesign

payload.raw_value_text: 90% of customers have switched from MetroCard to OMNY
payload.description: Nearly full adoption of tap-and-ride – 90% of customers have switched from MetroCard to OMNY

payload.claim_text: There is more service on 13 subway lines, including the A and the L. MTA is running more bus service, in large part due to the additional $35 million invested in the Queens bus system as a result of the bus network redesign. MTA is running more railroad service across the board.
payload.subject: MTA service levels
payload.statement: There is more service on 13 subway lines, including the A and the L. MTA is running more bus service. MTA is running more railroad service across the board.

warnings: claim.statement is accepted as passthrough, but prefer claim_text. Claim statement.

payload.description: Joint Finance Committee and Regular Board Meeting Minutes of the MTA and MTA Agencies
MTA about to implement a 4% fare increase
Shanifah Rieara, Demetrius Crichlow, and the Customer Service team have been out helping folks trade in their old MetroCards for free new OMNY cards
Board voted to adjourn the meeting at 11:26 am

Contract for New Fare Payment System; Modification to procure 20 Customer Service Point of Sale terminals. Cubic Transportation Systems.
Contract for the provision of software maintenance and support services related to the Intelligent Vehicle Network (IVN) system. Clever Devices LTD.

payload.claim_text: Award of a modification to a contract with Gannett Fleming Engineers and Architects (Contract PSC-19-3031) to provide for structural testing and analysis of four support buildings and construction support services for façade work. This modification will also extend the Contract term by 26 months, to May 30, 2027. 2. Award of a unilateral modification to a contract with Judlau Contracting (Contract A46026) to compensate the contractor for the direct costs of resequencing the work and ten months of impact costs, through October 20, 2026. This modification will also provide for an extension of the Substantial Completion date to October 20, 2026. 3. Ratification of a modification to a contract with Approved General Contractors (Contract 6484) addressing unforeseen site conditions.
payload.subject: MTA Capital Program

payload.description: Board approved the Joint Minutes of the MTA and MTA Agencies Regular Board meeting held on October 29, 2025

payload.claim_text: All the new vending machines for OMNY have been installed, and MTA is ahead of expectations in terms of the penetration of tap-and-ride among riders
payload.subject: OMNY implementation

payload.claim_text: Customers no longer have to put money on a MetroCard in advance. Customers do not have to pre-pay to get a $34 weekly or $132 for a monthly. Tap-and-ride is going to unlock a lot of new equity advantages but also new possibilities for us to address challenges of affordability
payload.subject: OMNY affordability and equity

payload.claim_text: We've never had a more pro-transit combo in City Hall and in Albany than we are looking at starting in 2026
payload.subject: Pro-transit leadership
payload.statement: We've never had a more pro-transit combo in City Hall and in Albany than we are looking at starting in 2026

warnings: claim.statement is accepted as passthrough, but prefer claim_text. Claim statement.
